target.name: Xsight Labs
target.founder: Guy Koren, Erez Shaizaf, Gal Malach

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $281M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $2.7B.

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Xsight Labs, a Series D semiconductor company founded by Guy Koren, Erez Shaizaf, and Gal Malach, has raised $281M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 private and 3 public peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including Tenstorrent, d-Matrix, and DEEPX at comparable stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $281M total raised as a proxy and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, implies a valuation of $422M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Tenstorrent at $2.7B, d-Matrix at $2.0B, DEEPX at $529M) yields a median of $2.0B, establishing a range floor of $529M and ceiling of $2.7B. The dispersion across peers reflects meaningful variability in market positioning and readiness.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $358M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.2B. The wide range reflects structural uncertainty in the frontier-chip market: stage-progression anchors conservatively near raised capital, while peer comparison suggests substantially higher valuations are market-supported at this stage. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to peers, customer traction velocity, and the ongoing capital intensity requirements for semiconductor development.
